name: The Agentic Era
summary: The current epoch. AI systems transition from tools that respond to prompts into autonomous agents that plan, use tools, write code, browse the web, and complete multi-step tasks independently. The Model Context Protocol (MCP) standardizes how agents interact with external systems. Computer use capabilities emerge. Multi-agent architectures allow specialized AI systems to collaborate. Self-improving AI research pipelines run hundreds of experiments autonomously. The question shifts from 'What can AI do?' to 'What should AI be allowed to do on its own?'
defining_characteristics: AI systems become autonomous agents, not just chat interfaces
Tool use and function calling become standard capabilities
Computer use — AI can operate GUIs like a human
Multi-agent architectures: specialized agents collaborating on complex tasks
Self-improving AI research: agents running experiments and learning from results
The alignment and control problem becomes urgent, not theoretical
